description:
You are a platform engineer maintaining several CI/CD pipelines.  
Inside the workspace two files already exist:

1. /home/user/pipelines/build-info.json  
2. /home/user/pipelines/README.txt (irrelevant for this task)

Your objective is to validate that **/home/user/pipelines/build-info.json** contains *all* of the following top-level keys:

• build_id  
• commit_sha  
• artifact_url  
• status  

Perform the validation using exactly **one shell command built around jq** (you may chain operators with &&, ||, ;, pipes, etc., but everything must be issued as a single command line).

Output requirements:

• Create the directory /home/user/ci_reports if it does not already exist.  
• Produce (or overwrite) the file /home/user/ci_reports/validation.log.  
• Write exactly one line (with a trailing newline) to that file:  
  • BUILD_INFO_VALID – if every required key is present.  
  • BUILD_INFO_INVALID – if one or more keys are missing.

The automated test will:

1. Confirm the file /home/user/ci_reports/validation.log exists.  
2. Read its first line.  
3. Accept the solution only if the content is exactly either “BUILD_INFO_VALID” or “BUILD_INFO_INVALID” followed by a single newline character.

Do **not** modify the original JSON file.  
Do **not** create any additional files.  
Remember: one concise jq-based command should accomplish the entire task.
truth:
Initial workspace (before the agent starts):

File: /home/user/pipelines/build-info.json  
Contents (4 lines, compact JSON):

{
  "build_id": "1024",
  "commit_sha": "9fceb02d131adf4d4f5aebd3233c2b5da4b8c4c6",
  "artifact_url": "https://ci.example.com/artifacts/1024/app.tar.gz",
  "status": "success"
}

Note: all four required keys are present; therefore the correct validation result is BUILD_INFO_VALID.

Verification script will check:

1. Presence of /home/user/ci_reports/validation.log  
2. Its exact byte content equals:  
   “BUILD_INFO_VALID\n”
name: task_000000_b456b484
